highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.154: Opus 4.8 & Dynamic Workflows Released
highlights.summary: Claude Code now defaults to Opus 4.8 with high effort mode, and you can use `/effort xhigh` for your most demanding tasks. The headline feature is dynamic workflows, which lets Claude orchestrate tens to hundreds of agents in the background so you can tackle larger, more complex coding projects automatically.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.152: /code-review --fix and Skills Improvements
highlights.summary: The `/code-review --fix` command now automatically applies review findings directly to your working tree, surfacing reuse, simplification, and efficiency suggestions without manual effort. The `/simplify` command now also invokes this under the hood, and skills/slash commands can now restrict which tools are available via `disallowed-tools` in frontmatter.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.8
highlights.summary: Anthropic has officially announced Claude Opus 4.8, the latest and most capable model in the Opus family, now integrated directly into Claude Code. If you want to understand what makes this model different and when to use it over lighter models, this announcement post is the place to start.

highlights.title: Dynamic Workflows in Claude Code
highlights.summary: This Anthropic blog post dives deeper into the new dynamic workflows feature, explaining how Claude can spin up and coordinate large numbers of sub-agents to complete complex, multi-step tasks on your behalf. It's a practical read if you want to understand when and how to delegate big projects to Claude Code rather than handling them step by step.

highlights.title: Claude Code as a Daily Driver: Claude.md, Skills, Subagents, Plugins, and MCPs
highlights.summary: This in-depth guide covers the core building blocks of a productive Claude Code workflow, including setting up your CLAUDE.md file, creating reusable skills and slash commands, using sub-agents for parallel work, and connecting plugins and MCPs. It's an excellent single-resource reference for developers who want to move beyond basic usage and customize Claude Code for their projects.

highlights.title: Learn 80% of Claude Code in 10 Minutes (2026 Tutorial)
highlights.summary: A fast-paced YouTube tutorial that covers the most important Claude Code features in just 10 minutes, making it ideal for developers who want a quick but comprehensive overview without committing to a longer course. Watch this first if you're new to Claude Code and want to get productive as fast as possible.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial takes a deeper dive into Claude Code over 30 minutes, covering intermediate and advanced features that go beyond the basics. It's a great next step after you've done a quick-start tutorial and want to understand more powerful workflows like custom commands and agent coordination.

highlights.title: Microsoft Starts Canceling Claude Code Licenses
highlights.summary: Reports indicate Microsoft is discontinuing Claude Code licenses it had bundled or provided to users, which may affect developers relying on employer-provided access. If your Claude Code access is managed through a Microsoft enterprise agreement, it's worth checking your license status and exploring direct Anthropic subscription options.

tips_of_the_day: Add a CLAUDE.md file to the root of your project with key context about your codebase, coding conventions, and preferred libraries — Claude Code reads this automatically at the start of every session, saving you from repeating yourself.

Use `/effort xhigh` before tackling complex refactors or architectural tasks to push Opus 4.8 into its highest reasoning mode; save it for genuinely hard problems since it uses more tokens than the default high-effort setting.

Try `/code-review --fix` on a feature branch after finishing a coding session — it will automatically apply simplification and efficiency improvements to your working tree, giving you a free second pass without any extra prompting.
